description: Versioned CI fleet manifest: maps every CI-enforced requirement to the named runner class that enforces it, its build flavor, tier, and red-X policy. A gate whose runner class is not yet provisioned is marked advisory (never silently green). Validated in CI by tools/check_fleet_manifest.py (the 'CI consumes it' tie); the human-readable companion is docs/ci-fleet-manifest.md.

gh-ubuntu-shared.notes: GitHub-hosted Linux. Shared, NOT perf-isolated — cannot host an R-QA-009 perf floor.

gh-macos-shared.notes: GitHub-hosted macOS. Doubles as the interim macOS build agent (R-BUILD-007); a dedicated agent is a follow-up.

gh-windows-shared.notes: GitHub-hosted Windows. The only MSVC /W4 /WX signal (the local dev gate is Ninja+GCC).

perf-linux-bare-metal.notes: R-QA-009 minimal-v1 perf box (one bare-metal Linux, median-of-5, +/-10% band). NOT yet provisioned -> every gate mapped here is advisory until it is.

determinism-matrix.notes: R-QA-005 / L-54 state-hash determinism matrix (min Linux-x64, Win-x64, macOS-ARM64). Realized on the GitHub-hosted shared trio: ubuntu-latest=Linux-x64, windows-latest=Win-x64, macos-latest=macOS-ARM64 (macos-latest is Apple Silicon). The determinism gate runs as a blocking named step in the 3-OS build job; the integer-only sim state + fixed-endian hashing make the golden hash portable, so a per-platform divergence turns that leg red. Provisioned M3 entry (issue #74).

gpu-runner.notes: REAL-GPU leg of the M4 visual-equivalence gate. Advisory until provisioned; the per-PR corpus gates run TODAY on software rasterizers (lavapipe + SwiftShader — the golden-scene-* gates).

minspec-desktop-proxy.hardware: R-QA-007 DESKTOP reference min-spec device: 2020 integrated-GPU ultrabook class — Intel Iris Xe (i5-1135G7-class), 16 GB RAM
minspec-desktop-proxy.notes: The named R-QA-007 desktop floor proxy (committed floor: minspec_floors.platforms.desktop). NOT yet provisioned -> the desktop floor gate is advisory; the per-PR methodology proxy runs on gh-ubuntu-shared + lavapipe (numbers advisory there too — a shared runner + software rasterizer is not this device).

minspec-web-proxy.hardware: R-QA-007 WEB reference min-spec device: the desktop min-spec class running latest stable Chromium (the v1 blocking browser)
minspec-web-proxy.notes: The named R-QA-007 web floor proxy (committed floor: minspec_floors.platforms.web). Advisory until provisioned; the per-PR render-web golden run (headless Chromium + SwiftShader) gates EQUIVALENCE, not perf.

n-daemons-host.notes: R-FILE-011 N-daemons-on-one-box scenario: validates composed watcher/fd/index-memory/cache budgets. Advisory until provisioned.

minspec_floors.methodology: R-QA-009: named proxy device (the runner_class rows above), median of >= 5 runs with dispersion recorded, ±10% variance band, results archived as a time series (CI artifacts).
minspec_floors.bench_subject: context_render_wgpu_offscreen bench — the representative T1 scene (lit3d: PBR + directional/point lights + shadow depth pass, src/render/lit/) rendered offscreen frame-after-frame; driver/gate: bench/minspec_floor.py.
minspec_floors.review_note: Initial floor commitments made at M4 exit (issue #141) within the R-QA-007/009 framework; the design docs name no device/rate, so these rows ARE the committed table — revise by reviewed PR, and ratify when provisioning each runner class.

desktop.reference_device: 2020 integrated-GPU ultrabook class — Intel Iris Xe (i5-1135G7-class), 16 GB RAM
desktop.ci_proxy: per-PR methodology smoke on gh-ubuntu-shared + lavapipe (render job): measure step BLOCKING (the subject must run green, results archived), floor gate ADVISORY until the runner class is provisioned.

linux-server.reference_device: 4-vCPU x86-64-v2 cloud/bare-metal Linux server, no GPU (the R-QA-009 perf-box class)
linux-server.ci_proxy: headless — no render floor (R-HEAD-002; the m4-exit-headless-no-render gate proves the render-absent path per PR). The 60 Hz fixed-timestep tick floor activates as a bench when the perf box is provisioned.

web.reference_device: the desktop min-spec class running latest stable Chromium (the v1 blocking browser)
web.ci_proxy: per-PR render-web golden run (headless Chromium + SwiftShader) gates T1 EQUIVALENCE, not perf; the web floor bench activates when the runner class is provisioned.

not_applicable.android: Trailing SHOULD — the Android floor activates when Android lands and never blocks a wedge milestone before then (R-QA-007 platform scope; R-BUILD-001).
not_applicable.ios: v2 — the iOS reference-device floor ships with iOS, v2's first deliverable (R-QA-007 platform scope).

gates.description: Deny-by-default dependency-license allowlist + SBOM emit (tools/check_licenses.py).
pytest over tools/tests + bench/tests (license gate, toolchain gate, corpus generator, perf gate, bench harness + daemons orchestration, budget-table extractor, fleet-manifest validator).
dev-preset build + ctest: the kernel, file-sync, and fault-injection-harness suites (M1-exit gate, R-QA-008).
dev-preset build + ctest on macOS (M1-exit gate, R-QA-008).
dev-preset build + ctest on Windows/MSVC (M1-exit gate + the only MSVC /W4 /WX signal).

M1 exit criterion 1 (issue #36): mutate a scene file via a CLI verb AND a raw text edit -> identical derived World headless, byte-identical derived state (ctest m1-exit-1-files-as-truth; runs on all three build-matrix legs via the 'M1 exit gate' step).

M1 exit criterion 2 (issue #36): a second CLI process attaches live to the running daemon over the real IPC wire via the capability-negotiation handshake (protocolMajor=0, hard-fail-on-mismatch) (ctest m1-exit-2-live-attach; all three build-matrix legs).

M1 exit criterion 3 (issue #36): real kill -9 of the real daemon process mid-multi-file-write, nothing lost — intent-log resume (HMAC-checked, re-jailed, re-CAS'd) + fresh snapshot on the new incarnationId (ctest m1-exit-3-crash-recovery; all three build-matrix legs).

M1 exit criterion 4 (issue #36): CLI ≡ RPC ≡ MCP ≡ introspection parity generated from the one registry — proven end-to-end over the real wire — plus the additive-only error-code catalog (R-CLI-008) (ctest m1-exit-4-contract-parity; all three build-matrix legs).

M1 exit criterion 5 (issue #36): a read/query-scoped token cannot install a package or trigger a build via DIRECT RPC — dispatcher-level denial mapping to catalog scope.denied, exit-class 6, verified over the real IPC transport (ctest m1-exit-5-scope-enforcement; all three build-matrix legs).

M2 exit criterion 1 (issue #68): author a scene from the CLI, save/reload with stable references — a GUID (composed identity, L-37) and an $entity ref (L-34) survive a restart (re-derivation from files, L-19); m2-exit-1 also spawns the real `context` binary (ctest m2-exit-1-cli-authoring; runs on all three build-matrix legs via the 'M2 exit gate' step).

M2 exit criterion 2 (issue #68): migrate a schema — a real component-payload version bump round-trips through both the parse-time (in-memory) and bulk `context migrate` (on-disk re-stamp + idempotence) paths, and a newer-than-current stamp is blocked (ctest m2-exit-2-schema-migration; all three build-matrix legs).

M2 exit criterion 3 (issue #68): flatten a composed scene into GUID-addressable content units (unit id = composed identity of the boundary root) that load/unload INDEPENDENTLY with sibling isolation — the co-designed chunk boundaries (ctest m2-exit-3-flatten-content-units; all three build-matrix legs).

M2 exit criterion 4 (issue #68): a composed-entity write lands in the correct file/level (default-outermost / --at-instance / --edit-template) and reports its winning-value-first provenance chain (ctest m2-exit-4-composed-write-provenance; all three build-matrix legs).

M2 exit criterion 5 (issue #68): a per-payload migration round-trips against the committed R-QA-011 fixture corpus (input->golden byte-exact + idempotence) through both document migration and the shared migrate_payload primitive the save-migration runner reuses (ctest m2-exit-5-per-payload-migration; all three build-matrix legs).

M2 exit criterion 6 (issue #68): the M2 data-model seam set as an executable audit — one assertion per seam (id-path overrides + innermost-out, composed write path, componentVersions selection, package-migration contract + set hash, override-path migration + orphans, $entity refs, composed identity, dup-id + re-key, schema vocabulary + units law, sidecar rules, scene-root singleton, save-migration groundwork, meta platforms reservation, override-hygiene) so a dropped seam turns the gate red (ctest m2-exit-6-seam-checklist; all three build-matrix legs).
ASan+UBSan preset build + ctest (ubuntu/clang; non-MSVC-only).
ThreadSanitizer preset build + ctest (ubuntu/clang; grows real coverage with M1 threaded kernel code).

M0 parse-bench FILE-FORMAT baseline on the 10k corpus + canonical fixpoint smoke + the R-QA-009 perf gate (advisory: a shared runner is NOT perf-isolated, so the numbers do not gate). The real-pipeline per-PR proxy is bench-attach-10k.

The ROADMAP-§6-tiering per-PR 10k proxy (issue #38) on the REAL attach pipeline (filesync reconcile index + derivation graph + daemon boot; `context bench` subject): fresh + index-warm attach, single-edit, bulk scenarios + the R-FILE-011 per-stage budget-table extraction. The JOB is blocking (the real path must run green end to end); the perf NUMBERS stay advisory until perf-linux-bare-metal is provisioned (the in-job perf gate is continue-on-error).
wasmtime + WAMR interpreter smoke on all 3 OSes (blocking legs).
WAMR AOT smoke + target-pin guard (blocking; un-quarantined 2026-07-04 — #24 SIGILL root-caused to host-CPU wamrc codegen, fixed by the x86-64-v2 pin in spikes/wasm/CMakeLists.txt).

The sandboxed WASM package-migration runner (src/runtime/wasm/, issue #71 PR3; L-37 / L-62 / R-SEC-009): the deterministic WasmRunner on the SHA-pinned wasmtime C-API prebuilt (Cranelift, consume_fuel, NaN canonicalization, relaxed-SIMD off/deterministic, threads off, fixed memory limit, fresh Store+instance per step; budget->fuel = kWasmFuelPerBudgetNode x max_nodes; fuel trap -> migration.budget_exceeded, other traps -> migration.step_failed). A CI-gated dependency path like cef-substrate (behind default-OFF CONTEXT_BUILD_WASM; the MSVC-ABI wasmtime.dll.lib cannot link under the local Strawberry-GCC dev gate, which builds the honest stub) — the 3-OS wasm-runner job flips it ON with CONTEXT_WASM_REQUIRE_RUNTIME=ON and runs the wasm-runner-* ctests (deterministic config, budget->fuel scaling, trap->catalog mapping, fuel-cost micro-validation, end-to-end document migration through real WAT guests). Windows rides the self-hosted MSVC runner. The committed .wasm fixture corpus + cross-OS determinism gate are PR 4 of #71.
WebGPU spike native build + offscreen render/readback self-check on all 3 OSes (software adapter; skips cleanly where none).
WebGPU spike Emscripten/emdawnwebgpu wasm+JS build (in-browser render is a local step).

M4 render foundation (src/render/, issue #114): the production T1 native backend (context_render_wgpu over the SHA-pinned wgpu-native prebuilt, R-SEC-009) builds on all 3 desktop OSes and the offscreen render/pixel-readback proof — driven through the rhi.h RHI abstraction (R-REND-001/002) — runs on the software adapter (lavapipe on ubuntu; skips cleanly where none; not registered on Windows). The library half (RHI abstraction + L-39 sim->render extract + double-buffer + R-HEAD-002 detachable renderer, context_render) is exercised by the build job's ctest with the wgpu toggle OFF. The real-GPU visual-equivalence gate (visual-equivalence, gpu-runner) stays advisory until that runner class is provisioned.

M4 render foundation, WEB leg (src/render/web/, issue #137 T6): the T1 RHI backend on the BROWSER's WebGPU via Emscripten + the emdawnwebgpu port (webgpu.h -> navigator.gpu; the L-56-locked web path, NOT a Dawn cross-compile). The SAME rhi.h implementation as the native render job (context_render_wgpu), compiled to wasm+JS from the standalone src/render/web target, rendering the same triangle (3D pipeline) + sprite (2D) T1 proofs. Since T7 (issue #141) the job also RUNS the harness in headless Chromium + SwiftShader WebGPU (tools/web_golden_run.py) — the M4 'one browser blocking' run gate; the collected frames feed golden-scene-web-chromium.

M4 golden-scene visual-equivalence corpus, NATIVE Linux-Vulkan leg (issue #141; ROADMAP §1 M4 exit): the render job dumps every corpus scene (triangle3d 3D pipeline + sprite2d 2D + lit3d representative lit scene + viewport 3D+2D composite + ui-hud runtime UI HUD, M7 a6/R-UI-005 + ui-worldpanel world-space RTT panel, M7 a9/R-UI-003/D4) through the wgpu-native backend on lavapipe and gates each against its committed goldens/ baseline with the named mean block-SSIM metric + per-scene tolerances (tools/golden_compare.py). Identical-within-T1 = same semantics, not bit-identical. Rebaselines are reviewed changes (goldens/README.md). BLOCKING per the minimal-v1 ruling: Linux-Vulkan + one browser.

M4 golden-scene visual-equivalence corpus, BROWSER leg (issue #141): the render-web job runs the built wasm harness in headless CHROMIUM (the v1 blocking browser — the only browser shipping WebGPU on headless Linux; the design record's reference browser since the M0 Tint/Chrome spike) over SwiftShader WebGPU, collects the kernel-free corpus frames (triangle3d + sprite2d + ui-hud, the M7 a6 runtime UI HUD) it POSTs back (tools/web_golden_run.py), and gates each against the SAME committed goldens/ baselines the native leg uses (tools/golden_compare.py). This closes the M4 exit's 'render 3D+2D desktop+web, identical within T1' criterion. BLOCKING per the minimal-v1 ruling; other browsers join as advisory rows when their runner classes are provisioned.

M4 exit criterion (issue #141): headless still runs with the render module ABSENT — under CONTEXT_BUILD_RENDER_WGPU=OFF (no GPU backend compiled), probing creates no device, no-adapter attach yields no Renderer, the engine steps 100 ticks through the extract/double-buffer loop renderer-less, and offscreen rendering is the OPT-IN, detachable path (R-HEAD-004): attach renders the corpus scene, detach returns to headless (ctest m4-exit-headless-no-render; runs on all three build-matrix legs via the 'M4 exit gate' step).

M5 exit criterion 1 (issue #168): the headless observer-editor WALKTHROUGH — open a project (compose a real scene, L-19) -> inspect (scene-tree F2 selection + inspector F3 + Problems F4 grouping/promotion) -> play it (F5 session-control over the edit-state view, L-51) -> make an override edit through the inspector (F3, L-20/L-30 CAS-guarded write) -> undo it (F7, replayed through the SAME path, R-HUX-001 no-clobber) -> the F1 viewport observes the SAME render::RenderSnapshot the play output produced (no second render path) composited via the L-41 handoff. Drives the LANDED CEF-free panels in process; the R-HUX-011 human-loops (selection / gesture->viewport / inspector-commit) are measured from instrumented timestamps (SHOULD, recorded in docs/human-latency-budget.md) (ctest m5-exit-1-walkthrough; runs on all three build-matrix legs via the 'M5 exit gate' step). The native-vs-web golden-scene SSIM equivalence is the sibling render / render-web job gate; the per-OS CEF boot smoke is the editor-cef-smoke job.

M5 exit criterion 2 (issue #168): the a11y scan + keyboard-only navigation gate — every registered editor panel is a11y-clean (uitree::audit_a11y: semantic/ARIA names, unique ids, every command keyboard-reachable) AND the a11y registry (registered_panels(), what the harness scans) exactly matches src/editor/gui/a11y/coverage.manifest.jsonl (the declared set tools/a11y_scan.py cross-checks), covering every M5 observer panel — placeholder + F1 viewport + F2 scene-tree + F3 inspector + F4 Problems + F5 playbar (this exit registers the historically-missing F4 Problems, completing the coverage manifest); F7 undo/redo's command panel is a11y-clean too (ctest m5-exit-2-a11y-coverage; all three build-matrix legs). The axe-class CEF-rendered DOM re-scan is the sibling editor-cef-smoke job gate (tools/a11y_scan.py, Linux blocking).

M5 exit criterion 3 (issue #168): the M5 observer-editor SEAM CHECKLIST as an executable audit (the milestone-closing mirror of m2-exit-6) — one assertion per M5 seam so a regression that quietly drops one turns the gate red: the R-EDIT-001 extension contract (deny-by-default trust boundary), the L-41 per-platform CEF surface handoff (never SendExternalBeginFrame), the headless UI-logic tree + a11y hook, the F2/F3/F4/F5/F1/F7 panel seams over the ONE bridge + the ONE `context set` write path (L-35/L-20/L-30/L-51/L-22/R-HUX-001), the F6 a11y coverage tie, the R-HUX-011 human-loop listener seams, and the error-catalog minting discipline (viewport.* + play.* are the reserved minted domains) (ctest m5-exit-3-seam-checklist; all three build-matrix legs).

M6 exit criterion 1 (design §M6-EXIT, issue #197): BOTH EXIT-A sample games (samples/roll-3d/ + samples/platformer-2d/, issue #194) build and headless-step N fixed ticks through the shipped RuntimeKernel session surface — moving/animated physics objects, particles emitting, audio events firing on miniaudio's NULL backend, injected input driving the player through the REAL context_input router — asserted against the R-QA-005 simTick counter and state queries. No GPU, no audio device; the cross-platform golden digests stay owned by the per-game smoke ctests (game-smoke-*). Runs in the blocking 'M6 exit gate' named step of the build job on all three build-matrix legs (ctest m6-exit-1-games-run).

M6 exit criterion 2 (design §M6-EXIT, issue #197): over a SUSTAINED run of the 2D game's authored TypeScript gameplay (samples/platformer-2d/scripts/movement.ts, transpiled by the pinned esbuild toolchain and executed in the REAL V8 host), the X1 GC-pause profiler channel (L-47, PR #189) shows EVERY JS-tier GC pause inside the R-SIM-008 inter-tick budget (a quarter of the fixed 60 Hz timestep — the `context profile gc` definition), fail-closed on any engine-side record loss (R-LANG-012 verdict). CI-only for its V8 dependency path (the local Strawberry-GCC dev gate builds the js stub and asserts the toolchain half only). Runs in the blocking 'M6 exit gate' named step of the build job on all three build-matrix legs (ctest m6-exit-2-gc-budget).

M6 exit criterion 3 (design §M6-EXIT, issue #197): the physics-active + animated + particle COMBINED gameplay scene's hierarchical state-hash is byte-identical on Linux-x64 / Win-x64 / macOS-ARM64 and the `context determinism diff` triage attributes injected divergences to the exact (tick, system, entity, componentField) — the EXIT ALIAS over the X3-hardened wedge gate (issue #192, PR #193): ctest m6-exit-3-determinism-wedge re-runs the SAME context_session_determinism_gameplay executable in the blocking 'M6 exit gate' named step, so the milestone exit formally blocks on the L-54 wedge law without duplicating the scene. The F0a deterministic:true attestation from actually-applied strict-FP flags stays gated by the deterministic job's blocking deterministic-attestation row (its hand-maintained --target list is unchanged).

M6 exit criterion 4 (design §M6-EXIT, issue #197): the L-48/R-NET-001 state-sync harness (src/runtime/netsync, PR #191) replicates the REAL roll-3d game's MOVING-BODY scene between two sessions — the source is the shipped 'roll-3d' RuntimeKernel session (the authority, its ball input-driven through the real router), the replica a bare kernel World that never runs physics — on the composed-id network identity + the dirty/delta replication metadata, and CONVERGES byte-identically (full snapshot first, the static floor culled from every later delta). Runs in the blocking 'M6 exit gate' named step of the build job on all three build-matrix legs (ctest m6-exit-4-netcode-harness).

M6 exit criterion 5 (design §M6-EXIT, issue #197): the M6 core-systems SEAM CHECKLIST as an executable audit (the milestone-closing mirror of m2-exit-6 / m5-exit-3) — one assertion per M6 seam so a regression that quietly drops one turns the gate red: the microkernel invariant (no src/kernel/ source includes a package header — L-60), every sim package composes on the ONE kernel World, the whole-build deterministic property (the attestation is PRODUCED from actually-applied strict-FP flags and fails closed — R-SIM-005), package sim-components fold into the hierarchical state hash by stable name, audio + cosmetic particles are presentation observers OFF the sim path (R-SIM-001), the input UI-vs-gameplay routing arbitration (R-SYS-007 / L-45), replication metadata bound to the composed id (L-48 / L-37), and the minter discipline (each package confined to its F0a-reserved catalog domain block) (ctest m6-exit-5-seam-checklist; all three build-matrix legs).

R-QA-007 min-spec floor bench, per-PR METHODOLOGY proxy (issue #141): bench/minspec_floor.py measure runs the representative lit3d scene offscreen at the committed floor resolution on lavapipe (median-of-5, dispersion recorded, results archived as artifacts — the R-QA-009 time series). The MEASURE step is blocking: the bench subject must run green end-to-end. The floor NUMBERS are enforced by the sibling advisory gate — a shared runner + software rasterizer is not the named proxy device.

R-QA-007 floor ENFORCEMENT on the per-PR proxy (bench/minspec_floor.py gate, ±10% band vs minspec_floors.platforms.desktop): continue-on-error until minspec-desktop-proxy is provisioned — reported and tracked, never silently green, never blocking on a number a shared software-rasterizer runner cannot honestly measure (R-QA-012 advisory-until-provisioned).

The committed DESKTOP min-spec floor (minspec_floors.platforms.desktop: Iris-Xe-class @ 60 fps, 1920x1080, lit3d subject, R-QA-009 methodology). Becomes a real blocking nightly floor when its named runner class is provisioned; until then the per-PR proxy pair above keeps the harness + numbers visible.

The committed LINUX-SERVER min-spec floor (minspec_floors.platforms.linux-server: 4-vCPU x86-64-v2 class @ 60 Hz headless fixed-timestep tick — no render floor, R-HEAD-002). The tick bench activates with the perf box; the render-absent path itself is gated per-PR by m4-exit-headless-no-render.

The committed WEB min-spec floor (minspec_floors.platforms.web: the desktop min-spec class in latest stable Chromium @ 60 fps, 1920x1080). Activates as a bench when its runner class is provisioned; per-PR the render-web golden run gates T1 equivalence, not perf.

Native shader cross-compile toolchain de-risk (src/render/shadercc/, issue #125, sub-task B of #119): glslang + SPIRV-Cross + SPIRV-Tools (the vcpkg `shader-crosscompile` manifest feature) build/link behind the default-OFF CONTEXT_BUILD_SHADER_CROSSCOMPILE flag, and a glslang->SPIR-V smoke (validated by SPIRV-Tools, reflected by SPIRV-Cross) runs — a CPU compile path (no GPU/native-render leg). Linux is the blocking build/link/smoke gate on the GH-hosted trio; macOS and Windows are advisory (continue-on-error) desktop-parity legs. Default-OFF means the main build matrix + local dev gate never resolve the toolchain. The real backend wires behind material/'s IShaderCompiler seam in sub-task C.

M5-F0a CEF build substrate (src/editor/cef/, issue #150; L-15 / L-41 / R-UI-007 / R-SEC-009): fetch + SHA-256-verify the pinned CEF prebuilt (tools/fetch_cef.py + tools/cef-prebuilt.json, verify-before-use fail-closed), build libcef_dll_wrapper, link a minimal headless boot substrate, and BOOT a CEF browser subprocess headless (`cef-substrate-boot` ctest -> exit 0). A CI-gated dependency path like render (behind default-OFF CONTEXT_BUILD_GUI_CEF): the MSVC/Clang-ABI CEF prebuilt cannot link under the local Strawberry-GCC dev gate, so the main build matrix + local dev gate never fetch CEF (the subdir early-returns when OFF). 3-OS matrix — Linux boots headless under xvfb (GPU disabled), Windows rides the self-hosted MSVC runner, macOS boots the bundled app. Build-SUBSTRATE smoke only (fetch/verify/link/boot), NOT the editor host boot smoke (M5-F0b). The pinned CEF archive is fetched once and version-keyed cached to bound CI cost.
CI consumes this manifest: tools/check_fleet_manifest.py validates it against the live workflow every run (self-consistency + advisory-until-provisioned rule).

Full 100k-file fresh + index-warm attach floor under the R-QA-009 methodology. Runs nightly as an ADVISORY TREND on gh-ubuntu-shared (bench-nightly.yml) until this perf-isolated runner class is provisioned — the runner_class names the class that makes it a real floor (then blocking, nightly).

Session-query latency budget (p99 <= 5 ms local) measured at the daemon's bounded service point (full JSON-RPC dispatch, socket excluded) against a 100k-file derived world. Advisory trend on the shared runner until the perf box is provisioned.

Sustained-write-load dirty-set latency + backpressure policy measurement (the DOCUMENTED maximum dirty-set latency of docs/latency-budget-table.md, under the library-default coalescing config). Advisory trend until the perf box is provisioned.

Dense-reference synthetic (R-FILE-011(e): ref edges O(refs) > O(files)) attach on the real pipeline — the edge-heavy case exercised nightly at proxy size (the property under test is edges >> files, not absolute count).

Multi-worktree scenario home (the harness `daemons` scenario: N real daemons + N attach clients on one box). The R-FILE-010 SHARED-CACHE contention measurement activates when the M2 derivation cache lands; advisory until the runner class is provisioned.

R-QA-005 / L-54 headless-session state-hash determinism (issue #74): a fixed seed + fixed injected input stream, stepped a fixed tick count, asserted against a cross-platform GOLDEN hierarchical state-hash (root + per-system + per-archetype + trace). Runs as the blocking 'Determinism gate' named step of the build job on ALL THREE build-matrix legs — ubuntu-latest (Linux-x64), windows-latest (Win-x64), macos-latest (macOS-ARM64) — so any per-platform divergence turns that leg red (ctest determinism-state-hash). The bisect/triage `context determinism diff` auto-triage is the reserved follow-up split. Since M6-F0a (issue #170) the family also carries determinism-physics-wedge (a fixed-point physics-shaped step, the physics-core determinism proof), and since M6 P1 (issue #174) determinism-physics3d-scene (a REAL physics-active scene — dynamic spheres + a rotated box ramp on a static floor — stepped through the context_physics3d package) — all run in this same blocking Determinism gate step.

R-SIM-005 / L-54 opt-in deterministic BUILD FLAVOR (M6-F0a, issue #170), anchored in R-SEC-009. The dedicated `deterministic` job proves the `deterministic` CMake preset CONFIGURES + BUILDS on the wedge trio — ubuntu-latest (Linux-x64, clang), macos-latest (macOS-ARM64, Apple clang), windows-latest (Win-x64, MSVC) — with the correct per-compiler strict-FP spellings (-ffp-contract=off vs /fp:strict), and that the build PRODUCES a verified deterministic:true attestation from the ACTUALLY-APPLIED flags (never a self-declared bit; the tamper/missing-flag paths fail closed with a determinism.attestation_* code). Runs the determinism-* family under strict-FP as the blocking 'Deterministic attestation + determinism gates' step: determinism-attestation (produces deterministic:true here vs an honest false on the default `build` job), determinism-state-hash, determinism-physics-wedge, and (since M6 P1, issue #174) determinism-physics3d-scene.

R-QA-006 agent few-shot corpus / R-BUILD-009 / R-QA-008 (issue #106): the maintained sample projects under samples/ build + headless-smoke-run through the REAL `context` binary (rots-if-broken), and the corpus is asserted to exercise the breadth of the R-CLI-009 registry surface — the stable one-shot CLI verbs (each certifying its RPC method + MCP tool by construction) plus every registered file kind (scene/camera, tilemap, string-table, project, ctx:replay). Runs as the blocking 'Samples corpus gate' named step of the build job on all three build-matrix legs (ctest samples-corpus). The M3-exit contract-freeze READINESS artifact — it does NOT perform the R-CLI-004 freeze; protocolMajor stays 0.

The REAL-GPU leg of the M4 golden-scene visual-equivalence corpus (goldens/ + tools/golden_compare.py): reruns the same corpus on real-driver hardware when the gpu-runner class is provisioned (advisory until then).
